problems.title: List Basics
problems.statement:
Practice the fundamental list operations, printing and checking the list after each step:
1) Create a list containing "Hari", "Keshava", "Madhava", "Yadava" and print it.
2) Append "Damodara" and print the list.
3) Pop the last item and print the list.
4) Pop the first item and print the list.
constraints: This problem takes no input — just print the list after each step, exactly as Python's print(list) shows it.
problems.starterCode:
# 1. create the list and print it
lst = ["Hari", "Keshava", "Madhava", "Yadava"]
print(lst)

# 2. append "Damodara" and print the list

# 3. pop the last item and print the list

# 4. pop the first item and print the list

problems.title: List Basics 2
problems.statement:
More list practice:
1) Create a list containing "Hari", "Keshava", "Madhava", "Yadava".
2) Insert "Narasimha" as the second item (right after "Hari") and print the list.
3) Append "Rama" and print the list.
4) Print only the first item.
5) Print the 3rd item.
problems.starterCode:
lst = ["Hari", "Keshava", "Madhava", "Yadava"]

# insert "Narasimha" as the second item and print the list

# append "Rama" and print the list

# print the first item

# print the 3rd item

problems.title: List Basics 3
problems.statement:
Loops and lists:
1) Create a list containing "Hari", "Keshava", "Madhava", "Yadava". Using a loop, print each word one by one.
2) Create a list with elements 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Using a loop, add 1 to each element, then print the resulting list.
problems.starterCode:
words = ["Hari", "Keshava", "Madhava", "Yadava"]
# loop through words and print each one

nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
# loop through nums, adding 1 to each element in place

# print the final nums list

problems.title: Sentence to Word List
problems.statement: Given a sentence, create a list containing all the words in it (use str1.split()), then print the list with print(word_list).
constraints: Words are separated by single spaces.
